name: ETH 4h Squeeze Persistence Release v1
edge_mechanism: compression releases directionally, but entry waits for persistence above fast structure rather than the first breakout bar
thesis: ETH 4h is the cleanest squeeze lane in the allowed basket because current regime is 100% chop. The edge is not the first breakout print. It is participating only once release persists beyond the initial bar, which should reduce false breaks while keeping enough density in a compressed market.
invalidation_condition: If ETH 4h keeps resolving squeezes with one-bar fakeouts and no follow-through even after a persistence filter, the mechanism fails.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: A compressed market transitions into expansion and proves that expansion by holding above fast structure for one more bar before continuation entry.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Pure chop assets spend time compressing. When volatility finally expands, persistence is more informative than the initial breakout bar, which is often noise.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: ETH 4h is the best fit because its regime is strongly chop, which is where squeezes form most naturally and persistence filters have the most value.

indicator_role_map.ADX_14: participation gate so expansion is not totally inert

pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: The persistence bar may arrive too late and convert decent breakouts into poor-location entries.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If entries exist but late-entry drag dominates, reduce the persistence tolerance before adding more trend filters.
pre_submit_reasoning.expected_outcome_pattern: Lower trade count than a raw breakout system, but cleaner expansions and fewer immediate reversals.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_success: ETH 4h produces non-sparse squeeze releases with better post-entry hold quality than prior breakout families.
pre_submit_reasoning.what_would_count_as_failure: Sparse signals, frequent late entries into exhausted moves, or PF still below cost drag.

density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: acceptable because squeeze plus one-bar persistence is simpler than prior ceremonial confirmation chains

test_lanes.why_included: Best-fit chop lane for compression and release behavior.
test_lanes.continue_if: ETH 4h shows enough squeeze releases and the persistence rule improves quality over raw breakout behavior.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: ETH 4h remains stuck in fakeout-heavy compression with no sustained release.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Moderate trade count and cleaner follow-through than first-break systems.

test_lanes.why_included: Checks whether the same persistence logic survives on the cycle primary lane.
test_lanes.continue_if: BTC 4h preserves squeeze-release behavior with similar hold quality.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: BTC 4h is too transition-heavy and turns squeezes into random churn.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Slightly lower density, larger expansions than ETH.

test_lanes.why_included: Adjacent timeframe test to see if the same release grammar gains density without losing all quality.
test_lanes.continue_if: BTC 1h raises participation and preserves post-persistence continuation.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: BTC 1h turns the concept into overtraded breakout noise.

test_lanes.why_included: Tests whether the same squeeze logic survives in a structurally more directional asset.
test_lanes.continue_if: TAO 4h shows fewer but stronger persistence releases.
test_lanes.rotate_away_if: TAO 4h proves the setup is redundant in an already trending market.
test_lanes.expected_if_right: Low-to-moderate count, higher asymmetry on winners.

long: Enter only if next bar closes >= EMA_20 and high exceeds prior bar high
short: Enter only if next bar closes <= EMA_20 and low breaks prior bar low

long: Time-based de-risk: cut 20% more after 6 bars if no 1.0 ATR expansion
short: Time-based de-risk: cut 20% more after 6 bars if no 1.0 ATR expansion
